title: Compression-Native Sharing Audit
research_question: Can the model save bytes by sharing stage-specific structure across similar layers instead of relearning similar transformations with separate weights or destroying them with naive low-rank factorization?
implementation_notes: Requires support for layer groups that share module weights or bases, plus small per-layer correction or scaling parameters.
This tranche is intentionally not another low-rank sweep; it tests sharing/reuse as the compression-native mechanism.
Saved bytes should be measured against the compressed submitted artifact, not just raw parameter count.

candidates.name: ab1-share-lower-two-mixer-weights
candidates.goal: Ask whether the earliest lower-stage transformations are repetitive enough to share directly.
candidates.hypothesis: The first lower mixer blocks may be doing similar enough work that separate weights are wasted.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the lower stage is structurally repetitive and overparameterized.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the cleanest first sharing test and attacks compression-native design without factorization.
candidates.description: share the full block weights across the first two lower mixer layers with per-layer scaling only

candidates.name: ab2-share-all-lower-mixer-weights
candidates.goal: Push the lower-stage sharing idea through the whole lower mixer band.
candidates.hypothesis: The whole lower mixer stage may behave more like repeated refinement than four distinct computations.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the bold version of the same compression-native question, not just a timid follow-up.
candidates.description: share block weights across all four lower mixer layers with per-layer scaling only

candidates.name: ab3-share-top-two-attention-weights
candidates.goal: Ask whether top reasoning layers are more repetitive than the current architecture assumes.
candidates.hypothesis: The top two attention blocks may be repeating a similar selection/refinement computation and can share most of their weights.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether upper refinement or lower structure is the better sharing surface.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the sharpest top-stage compression-native test that still respects the current frontier theory.
candidates.description: share the top two attention blocks with small per-layer scaling parameters

candidates.name: ab4-share-lower-then-widen-mixer
candidates.goal: Test whether sharing is more useful as reallocation than as pure savings.
candidates.hypothesis: If lower-stage sharing is viable, the saved bytes are better spent on a wider lower mixer than simply pocketed.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the right story is share-to-reallocate rather than share-to-save.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the most justified reinvestment run in the sharing family.
candidates.description: share the lower mixer stage and reinvest saved bytes into a wider mixer width

candidates.name: ab5-share-lower-then-add-upper-reasoner
candidates.goal: Test whether saved lower-stage budget is better spent on one more upper reasoning step.
candidates.hypothesis: Repeated lower-stage work may be overparameterized, and the best reinvestment is one extra upper reasoning layer rather than richer lower mixers.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether compression-native sharing should reallocate budget upward in the stack.
candidates.why_worthy: This asks the most important second-order question after any sharing success: what should the savings buy?
candidates.description: share the lower mixer stage and use the saved budget for one extra upper reasoning layer
